title: clematis smilacifolia subsp. munroiana
intro: Clematis smilacifolia subsp. munroiana, also known as Munro's clematis, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan regions of India and Nepal, characterized by its distinctive, white or pale purple flowers with five petals. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. Its health benefits include reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Munro's clematis typically involve infusions or decoctions of the roots or leaves, which can be consumed as teas or used as topical applications for skin and wound care.
